content: Current AI agent memory systems, which statically separate short-term and long-term storage, can be redesigned around the human brain's core principle that memory is not passive storage but an active part of the learning process. Just as the human brain strengthens, prunes, and updates old memories when encoding new information to support ongoing learning, AI agents could embed active learning directly into their long-term memory retrieval and consolidation steps.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
